Pearson Correlation Matrix

Hypothesis Tests

Caveat: All hypothesis tests assume independent observations. Hourly time-series data exhibits temporal autocorrelation, so p-values may be anti-conservative. Results should be interpreted as exploratory.
Welch's Two-Sample t-test
null_hypothesisNo difference in mean NO2_GT between high/low Temperature days
t_statistic0.8197404002259354
p_value0.41238506904378264
group_high_mean74.12039991491172
group_low_mean73.35290322580646
group_high_n4701
group_low_n4650
significant_at_0.05False
caveatIndependence assumption is violated due to temporal autocorrelation in hourly time-series data. p-value may be anti-conservative.
One-Way ANOVA
null_hypothesisEqual mean NO2_GT across all seasons
f_statistic17.59922564109715
p_value2.1749553929667305e-11
season_means
0 78.073380
1 71.141836
2 70.004755
3 76.554945
significant_at_0.05True
caveatANOVA assumes independent observations and homoscedasticity. Time-series autocorrelation violates independence; results should be interpreted cautiously.
Chi-Square Test of Independence
null_hypothesisHigh_Pollution and Humidity_Bucket are independent
chi2_statistic3.4480184501934925
p_value0.17834966864036333
degrees_of_freedom2
significant_at_0.05False
caveatChi-square assumes independent observations. Consecutive hourly readings are autocorrelated, violating this assumption. The test may overstate significance.

8. Limitations

  • Temporal autocorrelation: Sequential hourly measurements violate the independence assumption required by standard statistical tests and naive cross-validation. We use chronological train/val/test splits to mitigate this.
  • Single-site generalization: All data comes from one monitoring station in an Italian city. Model transferability to other urban environments is not established.
  • Sensor drift: Metal-oxide sensors are known to drift over time. The 12-month observation window may capture some drift effects.
